differential_diagnoses: Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) - granule cell neuronopathy variant
Leptomeningeal recurrence of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL)
next_steps: Send CSF for JC virus PCR (specifically looking for cerebellar granule cell neuronopathy)
Send CSF for VZV PCR and cytology for occult malignancy/lymphoma
Perform repeat lumbar puncture if initial flow cytometry/cytology were equivocal for high-grade malignancy
Check serum for paraneoplastic antibody panel (including anti-Yo, anti-DNER, anti-KLHL11)
Consult neurology, neuro-oncology, and infectious disease specialists
Consider brain biopsy if infectious and neoplastic studies are unrevealing

differential_diagnoses: Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) - granule cell neuronopathy (GCN) variant
Leptomeningeal recurrence of EBV-positive diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL)
next_steps: Perform CSF JC virus PCR (specifically requesting GCN-PML sensitivity)
Perform CSF VZV PCR and repeat CSF cytology with immunohistochemistry (e.g., CD20, CD3, EBER-ISH)
Consult Neuropathology to review existing CSF atypical cells and discuss potential brain biopsy utility
Perform whole-body PET/CT scan to assess for occult systemic relapse of lymphoma
Avoid empiric high-dose steroids until infectious and neoplastic etiologies are ruled out
Repeat lumbar puncture if cytology is non-diagnostic but clinical suspicion remains high for CNS lymphoma
